Remove broken blog_backend submodule reference to fix Netlify deploy

submodule reference and added blog_backend to .gitignore.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Unify all agents on state-interim, add anti-contamination ethics and Responder guidance

Retrospective fixes from Flight.htb engagement:

1. All agents now use state-interim MCP (previously only discovery agents).
   Technique agents can write critical mid-run discoveries (captured hashes,
   credentials) so the orchestrator sees them immediately via event watcher
   instead of waiting for agent return.

2. Added "do not crack" enforcement to all agent templates — agents save
   hashes via add_credential() and continue; orchestrator routes cracking
   to credential-cracking-agent in parallel.

3. Added Target Knowledge Ethics to all 11 agent templates — agents may
   use general pentest methodology from any source but must not use specific
   knowledge of the current target (e.g., HTB writeups for the box being tested).

4. Added Responder for NTLM Capture section to web-exploit-agent with
   explicit paths, flags, and daemon monitoring guidance (check logs via
   docker exec, not send_command/read_output).

5. Fixed deserialization-java skill's weak "Route to hash cracking" →
   "Route to **credential-cracking**".

6. Updated all docs (agents, architecture, engagement-state, mcp-servers,
   skills-reference, writing-skills) and state-server README/docstrings
   to reflect unified state-interim access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add LayerNorm after compression (bounded firing rates)

Raw compressed representations had unbounded magnitude from Qwen's large
hidden states, causing prediction loss to immediately saturate at clamp.
LayerNorm normalizes to unit variance, keeping MSE prediction loss ~O(1).

commit.message:
fix: prevent double-kick via synchronous ref guard

kicked state read stale closure value between rapid taps (touch+click),
allowing handleKick to fire twice before React re-rendered. Added
kickedRef for immediate synchronous check alongside the state.

commit.message:
feat: add self-healing resilience to Slack /abra pipeline

- Add fetchWithRetry() with exponential backoff for transient failures (429, 5xx)
- Graceful degradation: if embedding or brain search fails, answer with LLM general knowledge
- Ops alerting: critical failures (both LLMs down, missing env vars) notify Slack ops channel
- Retry on Slack response_url posting (prevents dropped responses)
- Fail-fast Claude (8s, no retry) → OpenAI fallback (15s, 1 retry)
- User always gets a response, never a silent failure
- Degradation notice when answering without brain context

SwarmCast v0.2.0 — Multi-agent weather prediction swarm

5 specialist AI agents independently analyze weather data, debate each
other's predictions, and converge on a confidence-weighted consensus.

Features:
- 5 agents with distinct forecasting personalities
- Live SSE streaming as agents complete analysis
- Agent debate round with revisions and challenges
- Reputation system scoring agents against actual outcomes
- 3-day severe weather outlook with CAPE profiles
- NWS alert integration and LLM threat analysis
- Morning weather brief in natural language
- Multi-location support with parallel forecasting
- Keyboard shortcuts, auto-scoring, comparison views
- Single dependency (express), zero build step

[Agent] feat(transfer-pak): wire gb_cart + transferpak via Obj-C++ bridge (#2791)

* feat(transfer-pak): wire gb_cart + transferpak via Obj-C++ bridge

- Add TransferPakSupport conformance to MupenGameCore (Swift layer)
- Implement m64p_media_loader callbacks (MupenGetGBCartROM/RAM) so the
  Mupen64Plus core can request GB ROM/save paths at runtime
- Call M64CMD_SET_MEDIA_LOADER in loadFileAtPath: after CoreStartup
- Add setGBCartROMPath:savePath:forPort: + getter methods to
  PVMupenBridge (ObjC layer) with proper C-string lifetime management
- Implement setMode:forController: (was commented-out stub)
- Expose Rumble Pak and Transfer Pak as selectable options in
  MupenOptions.swift (previously hidden behind Raw Data)

Part of #2736
Closes #2738

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address AI review — Transfer Pak bridge instance, memory leak, thread safety

- Use `bridge as! PVMupenBridge` (not `_bridge`) so TransferPakSupport
  sets paths on the same instance the media loader callbacks read from
- Free strdup-allocated _gbCartROMCStr/_gbCartSaveCStr in dealloc to
  eliminate memory leak
- Guard _gbCartROMCStr/_gbCartSaveCStr reads/writes with @synchronized
  to prevent data races between UI and emulator threads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix all remaining parseInt(contactId) crashes for manual contacts

Comprehensive audit found two more affected components:

VenueSuggestionPanel: parseInt(contactId) produced NaN for "manual-..."
IDs, causing the modulo filter to return [] — no venues shown for any
manually added contact (silent data bug, not a crash).
Fixed with the same idHash() char-code approach; bucket = hash %
totalContacts now correctly distributes venues across any contact ID.

ContactTimePicker: suggestedWindows.length would throw if the prop
arrived as undefined (possible before the CalendarPanel fix). Added
optional chaining (?.) as a defensive guard.

All three parseInt(contactId) sites in the codebase are now fixed:
- CalendarPanel.tsx (generateWindowsForDay) — previous commit
- ProposalScreen.tsx (generateMeetupSlots + getFriendRanks) — previous commit
- VenueSuggestionPanel.tsx (getVenuesForContact) — this commit

commit.message:
feat: use LLMArgumentExtractor for Java/JS arg extraction

Native Anthropic tool_use returns JSON types (true, {}) but Java/JS
need string representations ("true", "new HashMap<>(){{...}}").
Route Java/JS through LLMArgumentExtractor.extract() which has
language-specific prompting rules. Python still uses native tool_use.

Java 10-sample: 40% → 80%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add --chunk-days to auction_settlement to avoid daily API quota

KAMIS katSale API has a 10,000 calls/day limit. A full-year backfill
(365 days x 7 markets x ~5 pages) far exceeds this. --chunk-days N
caps processing to N days per run so each run stays within quota.
Recommended: chunk_days=50 per run, re-trigger daily for next chunk.

commit.message:
Phase 1.5: Gaussian parallel track — train, evaluate, visualize

- train_gaussian.py: trains PCA(128), AE(128), VAE(128), NF(128) entirely
  on Gaussian blur data (gauss_train.npz). Includes resume checkpointing
  every 10 epochs, early stopping (patience=30), and incremental CSV
  writing. Outputs gauss_results.csv with PSNR on ID/OOD1/OOD2 splits.
  Re-running safely skips dims with existing final checkpoints.

- evaluate_gaussian_interpolation.py: geometric consistency experiment
  (CoM, support, orientation vs alpha) using Gaussian-trained models and
  Gaussian anchor PSFs (Gauss_A: cx=22,theta=0 / Gauss_B: cx=42,theta=90,
  both sigma_maj=10, sigma_min=2). Outputs gauss_interp_geometric.csv,
  gauss_interp_alpha_kernels.npz, gauss_plot_interp_com.png,
  gauss_plot_interp_orientation.png.

- visualize_gaussian_interpolation.py: 4×5 dark-background gallery
  (Pixel/AE/VAE/NF × alpha=[0,0.25,0.5,0.75,1]) using afmhot colormap
  and per-row brightness normalisation. Outputs
  gaussian_interpolation_gallery.png.

- server_instructions.txt: appended full Gaussian track (G0–G3) with
  exact commands, expected outputs, and troubleshooting notes. Motion-blur
  and Gaussian jobs can be submitted to the queue simultaneously.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(spaces): route queries through space-aware Raft groups

The executor now routes data operations (insert, point get, scan,
update, delete) to the correct Raft group via hash(primaryKey) when
a table belongs to a multi-group space. Fan-out scans use N-way
merge-sort across all groups. Space caches are loaded on startup
and refreshed after CREATE SPACE / CREATE TABLE ... IN SPACE.
